scenario: Lattice Insurance models a Camunda 8.8 DMN decision table for SLA checks across 2500 cases per day. The modeler needs an input column that captures the time elapsed since case intake, expressed in days, hours, minutes, and seconds rather than in calendar years and months. The platform serves 12 tenants and processes 8400 transactions per day.
question: Which DMN type name is associated with the FEEL Days-Time-Duration type for that input column in Camunda 8.8?

options.text: dayTimeDuration is the DMN type name associated with the FEEL Days-Time-Duration type for time spans
yearMonthDuration is the DMN type name associated with the FEEL Days-Time-Duration type for time spans
dateTime is the DMN type name associated with the FEEL Days-Time-Duration type for elapsed time spans
time is the DMN type name associated with the FEEL Days-Time-Duration type for elapsed time spans

a.text: Correct. The DMN data types table in the docs pairs the DMN type name dayTimeDuration with the FEEL Days-Time-Duration type, which is the right fit for days, hours, minutes, and seconds elapsed since case intake.

b.text: Incorrect. The docs pair yearMonthDuration with the FEEL Years-Months-Duration type, not the Days-Time-Duration type used for day, hour, minute, and second spans.

c.text: Incorrect. The docs pair dateTime with the FEEL Date-Time type, which represents a point in time, not a duration of elapsed time.

d.text: Incorrect. The docs pair time with the FEEL Time type, which represents a time of day, not a duration measured in days, hours, minutes, and seconds.

explanation: The DMN data types table maps the DMN type name dayTimeDuration to the FEEL Days-Time-Duration type. For an input that records elapsed time in days, hours, minutes, and seconds, dayTimeDuration is the documented choice.
